description_long: This research institute consists of two large, rectangular buildings connected by an elevated enclosed walkway. The main building features a grid of windows and a flat roof, with a prominent entryway on one end. The secondary building is slightly taller and has a similar window pattern, with a long, narrow structure extending from its side. The surrounding grounds are landscaped with geometric pathways, grassy areas, and scattered trees, creating a campus-like environment. The exterior appears to be constructed from concrete and brick, with large glass windows providing natural light. The overall design is functional and institutional, with minimal decorative elements and a focus on utility.
description: A modern research institute with two connected rectangular buildings, grid-like windows, and landscaped grounds with pathways and trees.
description_view_0: This view shows both buildings, the connecting walkway, and the landscaped grounds with visible pathways and trees.
description_view_1: The front facade of the main building is visible, highlighting the grid of windows and the main entryway.
description_view_2: The side of the secondary building and the connecting walkway are prominent, with the grounds extending to the right.
description_view_3: The rear of the buildings is shown, with the landscaped area and pathways in the foreground.
attribute_entryway_design: The main entryway is a large, rectangular opening at the end of the main building, featuring double doors and a small overhang. The entry is accessible via a flat paved path, with no visible steps, suggesting wheelchair accessibility. The design is simple and functional, with minimal decorative elements.
